items.title: Anthropic launches Claude for Small Business via Claude Cowork
key_facts: 15 pre-built agentic workflows
15 repeatable skills
Integrations: QuickBooks, PayPal, HubSpot, Canva, DocuSign, Google Workspace, Microsoft 365
Deployed via Claude Cowork
User approval required before task execution
Pricing not disclosed
caveats: Connector support limited to 7 platforms at launch
Pro/Max plan includes AI training data opt-in by default (manual opt-out required)

items.title: Notion launches Developer Platform 3.5 with hosted Workers and External Agents API
key_facts: Workers: hosted Node/TypeScript sandbox, free through August 2026
External Agents API: Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, Decagon native integrations
External Agents API in private beta (waitlist)
Database Sync: Zendesk, Salesforce, Postgres and other API-backed systems
CLI (ntn) available on all plans
caveats: External Agents SDK is alpha — not production-ready
Pricing per Worker invocation post-August not announced
Supported languages and resource limits undocumented

items.title: Amazon ships Alexa for Shopping, combining Alexa+ and Rufus
key_facts: Merges Alexa+ and Rufus into single shopping assistant
Available on Amazon mobile app, website, Echo Show devices
Core features available without Prime membership
'Buy for Me' cross-site purchasing requires Alexa+ subscription
3x purchase completion rate vs original Alexa (vendor-claimed)
caveats: 3x purchase rate is vendor-measured, no independent replication
Alexa+ subscription pricing not confirmed in available sources

items.title: OpenAI connects ChatGPT to financial accounts via Plaid for US Pro subscribers
items.tier_nuance: Primary URL returned HTTP 403; content confirmed via TechCrunch, MacRumors, 9to5Mac, and Plaid blog
key_facts: Pro subscribers in US can connect 12,000+ financial institutions via Plaid
Read-only access: balances, transactions, investments, liabilities
Cannot see full account numbers or execute transactions
Account data deletion takes 30 days after disconnecting
Dashboard shows spending, subscriptions, upcoming payments, portfolio
200M users already ask financial questions to ChatGPT monthly (vendor-claimed)
caveats: Primary URL inaccessible from run environment
Pro subscribers, US only at launch
Data use beyond AI training not clearly documented
Active class action lawsuit for prior data-sharing with Google/Meta
Significant public backlash on Hacker News

items.title: PwC deploys Claude to hundreds of thousands of professionals, launches Office of the CFO unit
items.tier_nuance: Performance claims (70% improvement, underwriting timeline) are vendor-claimed; no independent audit
key_facts: Roll out to US teams first, then global workforce of hundreds of thousands
30,000 professionals to be trained and certified on Claude
Joint Center of Excellence established
Office of the CFO new business unit anchored in Anthropic technology
Targets regulated industries: banking, insurance, healthcare
caveats: All performance figures self-reported in co-authored announcement
'Hundreds of thousands' is a global target, not current deployment count
70% delivery improvement and 10-week → 10-day underwriting are client case claims, not audited

items.title: Anthropic and Gates Foundation commit $200M to global health, education, and economic mobility AI
key_facts: $200M in grants, Claude credits, and technical support over 4 years
Domains: global health, life sciences, K-12 education (US, sub-Saharan Africa, India), economic mobility
Public goods: benchmarks, datasets, evaluation frameworks for healthcare AI

items.title: Cerebras Systems raises $5.55B in Nasdaq IPO, closes first day at ~$95B market cap
key_facts: Ticker: CBRS on Nasdaq
30M shares at $185, raised $5.55B
Largest US tech IPO since Uber 2019
Closed +68% at ~$311, market cap ~$95B
$510M 2025 revenue, 47% net margin
OpenAI compute deal 750MW expiring 2028
Stock fell ~10% on day 2 (May 15)

items.title: Recursive Superintelligence emerges from stealth with $650M at $4.65B valuation
key_facts: $650M raised, $4.65B valuation
Round led by GV and Greycroft, Nvidia and AMD participating
Round oversubscribed
Founders: Richard Socher (ex-Salesforce CSO) and Tim Rocktäschel (UCL / ex-DeepMind)
London HQ, San Francisco presence
Fewer than 30 employees, no shipped product
Mission: self-improving AI through open-ended algorithms
caveats: No shipped product; 4 months of operating history
'50,000 doctors' capability claim is pre-product marketing, no benchmark

dropped_items.reason: Published May 12 — 1 day outside strict 72h window

dropped_items.title: ServiceNow AI Control Tower expansion at Knowledge 2026
Sakana AI RL Conductor / Fugu Beta
Broadridge agentic post-trade capabilities
dropped_items.reason: Could not confirm scope of 'production-ready' claim from primary source within run

dropped_items.title: Microsoft Agent Framework Building Blocks Part 3
dropped_items.reason: Could not confirm publication date within May 13-16 window

conflicts_surfaced.description: OpenAI presented read-only access and no account-number visibility as privacy reassurances without addressing an active class action lawsuit alleging prior unauthorized data sharing with Google and Meta. Hacker News thread showed significant practitioner skepticism (>200 comments). Trust gap is real but unresolved.
conflicts_surfaced.reconciliation: Privacy claims are vendor-stated, unverified. No independent security audit available at time of publication.

exploration_stats.sea_search_result: Background reporting on Indosat Sahabat AI (May 7) and Indonesia AI ecosystem; no in-window primary announcements from region
